articles.title: Tensor-polarized parton distribution functions of the deuteron by a convolution model
articles.raw_summary: arXiv:2607.09237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tensor-polarized parton distribution functions (PDFs) are calculated for the deuteron by using a convolution formalism, where the tensor-polarized PDFs are given by the corresponding nucleon's unpolarized PDFs convoluted with the tensor-polarized nucleon momentum distribution in the deuteron. These distributions are obtained at $Q^2=2.5$ GeV$^2$ in order to compare with the tensor-polarized PDFs which were determined by HERMES $b_1$ data. The obtained distributions are very different from the ones determined from the HERMES data, which indicates further studies are needed to clarify the difference, possibly by considering a new mechanism beyond the simple bound system of a proton and a neutron. The obtained PDFs $\delta_T q $ and $\delta_T \bar q$ are converted to the PDFs of the Trento convention $f_{1LL}^{\, q}$ and $f_{1LL}^{\, \bar q}$, and they are used for estimating the twist-3 PDFs $f_{LT}^{\, q}$ and $f_{LT}^{\, \bar q}$ by using a Wandzura-Wilczek-like relation. articles.title: Leptogenesis with sub-electroweak-scale reheating temperature
articles.raw_summary: arXiv:2607.09282v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the generation of the baryon asymmetry of the Universe via leptogenesis during the post-inflationary reheating epoch, considering reheating temperatures below the temperature of sphaleron freeze-out temperature. Within the framework of a monomial inflaton potential during reheating, we analyze three perturbative reheating scenarios in which the inflaton decays into (i) a pair of SM-like bosons, (ii) a pair of SM-like fermions, or (iii) exclusively into a pair of heavy right-handed neutrinos. For each case, we identify the regions of parameter space that successfully reproduce the observed baryon asymmetry consistently tracking the sphaleron interaction rate during reheating, while satisfying existing cosmological constraints. We also highlight the potential of future primordial gravitational wave observations to probe this class of scenarios.

articles.title: Diffusion Monte Carlo study of deuteron-like fully light hexaquarks
articles.raw_summary: arXiv:2607.09288v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We perform a Diffusion Monte Carlo study of fully light hexaquark containing three $u$ quarks and three $d$ quarks within a constituent-quark model. Both compact and baryon--baryon-like arrangements were considered separately. All compact hexaquark configurations are found well above their corresponding baryon--baryon thresholds, suggesting that deeply bound compact six-quark states are not favored in the light-quark sector within this model. By contrast, several dibaryon-like configurations lie close, but above, to the $NN$, $N\Delta$, and $\Delta\Delta$ thresholds and show spatial structures compatible with molecular states. One configuration exhibits two well-defined nucleon-like subclusters separated by several femtometers, closely resembling the spatial structure of the deuteron, although its calculated energy remains slightly above the corresponding threshold.

articles.title: Running coupling effects in the anti-collinear resummation in high energy evolution
articles.raw_summary: arXiv:2607.09337v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the effects of the running of the QCD coupling on the anti-collinear resummation in JIMWLK evolution in the linear (BFKL) regime. We determine the appropriate scale choice for the coupling entering the JIMWLK kernel, and derive the anti-collinearly resummed BFKL kernel, which includes running-coupling effects both in the resummation equation (i.e. DGLAP) and in the JIMWLK kernel proper. We find that the running of the coupling generally further slows down BFKL evolution, as expected. Surprisingly however the value of the generalized characteristic function at $\gamma=1$ is unaffected by the running coupling owing to subtle cancellations. We develop an approximation that allows us to use the generalized characteristic function to study the BFKL Green's function. Within this approximation we find that the Pomeron intercept in the anti-collinear regime is significantly reduced by both, the resummation and the running of the coupling.

articles.title: A Window onto New Invisible Particles via Semi-Visible Higgs Decays
articles.raw_summary: arXiv:2607.09344v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Searches for new physics continue at the LHC in several forms, including new-particle searches, precision measurements of SM couplings, and searches for signals of new invisible particles. In this talk, we discuss the reach in parameter space of new invisible particles with the semi-visible Higgs decay modes $H\to \ell^+\ell^- + ~\rm E{\!\!\!/}_T$ and $H\to jj + ~\rm E{\!\!\!/}_T$. We first parametrise the new invisible particles and their interactions with the SM through an effective field theory at dimension six. We then study the respective signals and the corresponding background, finding small signals with large backgrounds. We find, however, that the kinematics of these processes are sufficiently rich to allow a signal extraction that we first quantify with a cut-based analysis and later with a multivariate BDT.

articles.title: Reserve Bank convenes Third Annual Conference of Internal Ombudsmen
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) organised the third annual conference of Internal Ombudsmen (IO) 1 on July 13, 2026, in Mumbai. The Conference brought together IOs from across a representative spectrum of regulated entities including banks, non-banking financial companies (NBFCs), credit information companies (CICs) and other regulated entities, along with the Managing Directors and Chief Executive Officers, Executive Directors in charge of customer service, Principal Nodal Officers as well as RBI Ombudsmen and senior officials of the Reserve Bank. In his keynote address, Shri Swaminathan J., Deputy Governor, exhorted the IOs to provide a meaningful, fair and effective resolution of customer complaints. The Deputy Governor also requested the IOs to identify recurring issues, undertake root cause analysis and help implement remedial measures. He added that an effective IO mechanism should reduce external escalation and urged Boards and senior management to empower the IO mechanism and use its insights to strengthen customer service and grievance redress. Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited A Govt. of India (Ministry of Railways) Enterprise
DFCCIL Overview
articles.extracted_text: The Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited is India’s most ambitious and transformative infrastructure initiative undertaken by Indian Railways to modernize freight transportation and strengthen India’s logistics backbone. Conceived as a long-term structural solution to chronic congestion, declining rail share in freight movement and rising logistics costs, DFCCIL is creating high capacity, high speed, freight only rail corridors that are redefining how freight move across the country and supporting India’s journey towards sustainable, competitive and resilient logistics transportation. Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) Length: 1,337 km Route: Ludhiana (Punjab) – Sonnagar (Bihar) Core Role: Freight corridor for energy and manufacturing sectors Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) Route : Dadri (Uttar Pradesh) – JNPT (Mumbai) Core Role : High-capacity container logistics corridor Key Connectivity To strengthen first- and last-mile connectivity, DFCCIL is enabling development of Gati Shakti Cargo Terminals (GCTs) along the corridors. DFCCIL is one of the key driver of India’s green logistics transition, enabling a shift from road to rail through energy-efficient, high-capacity freight corridors. With significantly lower emissions, the Dedicated Freight Corridors support cleaner and more sustainable freight movement. DFCCIL promotes the use of renewable energy, contributing to long-term environmental sustainability , to further reduce its carbon footprint and advance India’s Net Zero goals. Engineering Marvels and Iconic Structures The Dedicated Freight Corridor network stands out as a landmark in modern railway engineering, delivering complex infrastructure across challenging geographies, dense urban stretches and environmentally sensitive zones. These corridors are engineered for heavy haul freight, high axle loads and long, high frequency trains, demanding a new benchmark in bridge design, tunnelling, alignment geometry and construction methodology. Iconic structures such as the Sohna Tunnel enable seamless freight movement through difficult terrain and advanced safety systems, while the Kalamboli Girder Bridge on the WDFC showcases precision engineering in a congested suburban environment without disrupting urban mobility. The Yamuna Rail Bridge near Dalelpur, Uttar Pradesh is one of India’s longest rail bridges, carries heavy haul freight traffic across the Yamuna River. DFCCIL provides a structural solution by creating dedicated, high-capacity corridors that separate freight from passenger traffic, ensuring faster, predictable and reliable movement of goods while releasing capacity on the existing network for passenger services. Project Funding and Financial Sustainability Beyond logistics efficiency, the Dedicated Freight Corridors have emerged as a powerful enabler of national priorities under the ‘PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan’. The corridors actively support flagship initiatives such Make in India, Start Up India, the Export Promotion Capital Goods Scheme and broader export promotion efforts by providing reliable, cost-effective logistics to manufacturing clusters and MSMEs. DFCCIL is instrumental in realising the vision of Aatmanirbhar Bharat by strengthening domestic supply chains, improving export competitiveness and integrating Indian industry with global value chains. As India aspires to become a developed nation by 2047, the DFCCIL stands as one of the foundational infrastructure pillar d

articles.title: Sonnagar - Dankuni
articles.extracted_text: Sonnagar- Dankuni Section The Sonnagar–Dankuni section, originally part of the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), spans approximately 538 km and traverses three states: Bihar, Jharkhand, and West Bengal. It passes through two zonal railways: East Central Railway (ECR) and Eastern Railway (ER). In 2022, the Sonnagar–Andal section was sanctioned as a multi-tracking project under the EPC mode and subsequently handed over to ECR and ER. The Andal–Dankuni section, covering 163 km, lies entirely within the state of West Bengal and falls under the jurisdiction of Eastern Railway. This section includes two junction stations (New Khana and New Dankuni), two crossing stations (New Rajbandh and New Jaugram), and one cabin at Andal East. As of now, approximately 97% of the required land for this section is under possession.

articles.title: New Corridor
articles.extracted_text: The proposed corridor stretching 1080 km traverses through three states of India, namely West Bengal, Orissa, and Andhra Pradesh. Mostly runs parallel to the existing Kharagpur-Vijayawada coastal railway line. It also passes over three zonal railways, namely South Eastern, East Coast and South-Central Railway. The DPR for this corridor is likely to be completed by Dec’22. Traffic Potential: The major traffic generators which will be contributing to East Coast Corridor in PIA are mines of Coal, Iron ore, Thermal Power Plants, Ports, Fertilizers, POL, CONCOR ICDs, FCI godowns, etc. 2. East West Corridor :- The proposed East-West DFC (EWDFC) from Bhusaval to Dankuni including spur line from Rajkharswan to Andal passes through five states namely West Bangal, Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, and Maharashtra. The length of the mainline is 1551 RKM and the length of the spur line is 187 RKM (Total: 1738 RKM). This corridor has further been extended from Bhusaval to Palghar. The DPR for this corridor is planned to be completed by Mar’23. Traffic Potential: The major traffic generators which will be contributing to East-West Corridor in project influence area (PIA) are Mines of Coal, Iron ore, bauxite, Manganese, Ferro Alloys, Steel Pants, Ports – major and minor, Fertilizers, Thermal Power Plants, Petroleum Oil Lubricants (POL), Cement plants, Container Corporation (CONCOR) Inland Container Deports (ICDs), Food Corporation of India (FCI) godowns etc. 3. North-South Corridor :- Traffic Potential: The major traffic generators which will be contributing to North-South Corridor in Project influence area (PIA) are Mines of Coal, Cement plants, Thermal Power Plants (TPP), Petroleum Oil Lubricants (POL), Container Corporation (CONCOR) Inland Container Deports (ICDs), Food Corporation of India (FCI) godowns, Fertilizers etc.

UTTARAKHAND (Metro Rail News): Rail Vikas Nigam Limited (RVNL), under the Ministry of Railways has awarded ₹263 crore contract to Siemens for the overhead rail electrification work of the 102 km Rishikesh-Karnaprayag rail line in Uttarakhand. This was announced on Monday, 13 July 2026 via an official press release by Siemens. The Rishikesh-Karnaprayag line is a new broad-gauge railway totaling 125 km, with extensive tunneling (over 100 km of tunnels) through the Himalayas. It is expected to significantly reduce travel time and improve access to Uttarakhand’s religious and tourist destinations. #PressRelease | Powering progress in rail. Siemens is enabling the 102 km Rishikesh–Karnaprayag corridor with advanced electrification for reliable, future-ready mobility. Read more: https://t.co/x6biWhO2Hf pic.twitter.com/dvKainuscb The scope of work includes supply and installation of Rigid Overhead Catenary Systems (ROCS) and Flexible Overhead Catenary Systems (FOCS) across tunnels, bridges, yards, and open routes. Also includes powering a corridor with 90% tunnels and one nearly 10 km long. The purpose of the electrification is part of the Char Dham Railway Project to provide all-weather connectivity to major pilgrimage sites (Kedarnath, Badrinath, etc.) and boost tourism and economic development in the region. Mr Rajeev Joisar, CEO – Mobility Business, Siemens Limited, expressed their happiness to partner with RVNL for this prestigious project, which is a significant step towards enhancing sustainable and reliable connectivity in one of the most challenging terrains in India. He further stated that this order further reinforces Siemens’ leadership in advanced rail electrification technologies. Also Read: Karnataka’s CM outlines 500-km Bengaluru Metro expansion plan

BENGALURU (Metro Rail News): A massive 500-kilometer expansion of Bengaluru Metro is set to anchor the Karnataka government’s newly announced ₹1.5 lakh crore mega-budget for Bengaluru’s urban development. Unveiled at the ‘KATALYST CONNECT’ summit on July 8, 2026, this massive transit push aims to support the state’s aggressive goal of drawing 500 new Global Capability Centres (GCCs), creating 3.5 lakh high-quality jobs by 2029. While the mega budget also funds the Bengaluru Business Corridor, new tunnels, and relaxed vertical building limits (FAR), the expanded metro network takes center stage to guarantee seamless urban mobility for the state’s rapidly growing tech workforce. Bengaluru Metro: The 500-KM Roadmap to Power Karnataka’s Tech Future The Karnataka government has positioned the expansion of ‘Namma Metro’ as one of the core pillars of its urban growth strategy. Framed alongside the state’s “Beyond Bengaluru” initiative, these transit projects aim to handle heavy city traffic while building the necessary infrastructure to support massive investments from Global Capability Centres (GCCs). The Immediate Target: 175 KM by December 2027 Chief Minister Shri DK Shivakumar announced that the state is aggressively pushing to double its currently operational 96 km network. The target is to have 175 kilometers of active metro lines fully operational by December 2027. Looking further ahead, the government has conceptualized a massive 500-kilometer long-term expansion plan consisting of new elevated routes and interconnected underground tunnel corridors. This mega transit footprint is backed by a comprehensive ₹1.5 lakh crore city infrastructure budget designed to tackle urban congestion. Work is progressing rapidly on Phase 2 corridors to connect major business and IT hubs. The Pink Line (Kalena Agrahara to Nagawara): The elevated portion of this crucial line is scheduled to open first by August 2026, with the entire corridor—including its complex underground sections—targeted for a full launch by March 2027. The Blue Line (ORR & Airport Extension): Construction is moving forward along the Outer Ring Road (ORR) segments, heading toward a dedicated direct connection to Kempegowda International Airport, which is scheduled to scale up operations through mid-to-late 2027.This is a Rs.15000 Crore project covering 58 kms. Phase 3: India’s Longest Double-Decker Corridor Approved by the Union Cabinet, Phase 3 focuses entirely on fixing traffic bottlenecks across Western Bengaluru. This section will feature India’s longest double-decker metro corridor, stacking elevated metro tracks directly above multi-lane vehicular flyovers to optimize limited road space. ‘‘Our Governm

KOLKATA (Metro Rail News): Taking a significant step towards introducing driverless operations, the Commissioner of Railway Safety (CRS), Northeast Frontier Circle Sumeet Singhal conducted an inspection on the East–West corridor (Green Line) from the Howrah Maidan station terminal to Salt Lake Sector V, which includes India’s first underground metro tunnel spanning 520 metres under the river, allowing the trains to cross the river in 45 seconds. The CRS wanted to ensure that all necessary arrangements were taken care of to handle any emergency occurrences during the driverless operations under the river. The services on this route began at 4:30 pm on 12th July, instead of 9 am for the inspections. CRS Inspector Sumeet Singhal also visited the Central Park maintenance-cum depot to inspect the OCC, which serves as the brain of Communications-Based Train Control (CBTC). The train halted at all 6 elevated and 6 underground green line stations. The train was frequently stopped between stations to check how the system would act in emergency situations. Following the inspection, once the official permissions from CRS are granted, the Kolkata Metro Rail Corporation can begin driverless passenger operations on the 16.6 km corridor. NEW DELHI (Metro Rail News): In an announcement on Sunday (July 12), the Union Minister of Housing and Urban Affairs, ML Khattar proposed that air spaces above Delhi Metro should be utilized for residential buildings. The announcement was made during the minister’s speech at the Central Public Works Department (CPWD)’s 172nd foundation day. The minister said that “The use of air spaces for housing purposes is a new thing that many countries are implementing”. He referred to a hotel in Gandhinagar which is developed above a metro station as an example for the proposal. He said that a similar project is being thought about for Delhi. Khattar further said that he has sought Delhi Development Authority (DDA) to find ways to implement the project. Moreover, he also encouraged CPWD to work on the project and find ways to utilize linear projects for air spaces. The minister said that if we use the air spaces by developing pillars on the sides, then the project could be implemented at a low cost. He said that people will get affordable housing and the metro will also be benefited with this proposal. It can become a major source of non-fare revenue for the metro system. The step can boost the revenue of Delhi Metro and bring the possibilities of attaining financial sustainability through the proposed property model. The project can also be impactful in the housing and congestion issues in the region. A global counterpart of Delhi Metro, the Hong Kong Metro system has shown remarkable growth through its property model. The Hong Kong Mass Transit Railway (MTR)’s “Rail+Property” model is well-known for its financial sustainability. The metro stays in profit and a major reason behind that is the proportion of revenue it generates from real estate. MTR manages several properties above their metro stations and depots. Also Read: Adarsh Nagar Railway Station approved as new stop for Kalka and Himachal Express Trains

DELHI (Metro Rail News): In a major relief for rail passengers in North and West Delhi, the Ministry of Railways has approved stoppages for the Delhi–Kalka Express (Train No. 14331) and the Delhi–Daulatpur Chowk Himachal Express (Train No. 14053) at the Adarsh Nagar railway station. The Delhi Chief Minister’s Office (CMO) issued the official public announcement on Sunday 12 July 2026 through their official social media and statements. This initiative will provide easier and faster connectivity to major tourist and religious sites across Himachal Pradesh, Chandigarh, Kurukshetra, Fatehgarh Sahib, and Anandpur Sahib. The new stoppages were approved following a formal request from Delhi Chief Minister Smt. Rekha Gupta to Union Railway Minister Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw. Smt. Rekha Gupta stated that the Delhi government is prioritizing citizens’ daily needs with this new halt, which will save time for residents of North and West Delhi by eliminating the need to travel to the New Delhi or Old Delhi railway stations. She further expressed her gratitude to Union Railway Minister Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw and PM Shri Narendra Modi for their efforts in making this public welfare decision. MAHARASHTRA (Metro Rail News): MahaRail (Maharashtra Rail Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited) officially unveiled 9 key infrastructure projects across six districts of Maharashtra on Sunday, July 12, 2026. These projects represented a combined investment of approximately ₹484 crore. 🔸Inauguration of 4 Road Over Bridges (ROBs), 4 Underpasses (Subway) and 1 Road Under Bridge (RUB) across Maharashtra jointly developed by Maharashtra Rail Infrastructure Development Corporation (MahaRail), the Government of Maharashtra and the Ministry of Railways, at the hands… pic.twitter.com/Hn2FnyCCAS The inaugural ceremony was planned and executed as a dual-format event, combining physical attendance with a state-wide virtual broadcast at the newly constructed Deepti Signal Road Over Bridge (ROB) in Nagpur. The Chief Guest of the ceremony was Nitin Gadkari (Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways), while the Presiding Guest was Devendra Fadnavis (Chief Minister of Maharashtra). The projects included the Deepti Signal ROB-the main 1,320-meter flagship bridge built at Railway Crossing No. 73 in Nagpur-and eight other projects located across the Satara, Nashik, Sangli, Amravati, and Chandrapur districts of Maharashtra. The Indian Railways played a core role in these projects, acting as both a structural partner and an operational regulator. Because the infrastructure involved crossing active train tracks, the entire initiative was built on a foundation of federal cooperation. In total, the infrastructure consisted of four road over bridges (ROBs), four subways, and one road under bridge (RUB), combining for a total cost of around ₹484 crore. Out of these nine projects, seven were unveiled under a 50:50 co-financial agreement between the Ministry of Railways and the Government of Maharashtra. These projects were expected to result in much safer, faster, and more efficient transportation across the state. articles.title: Piero Cipollone: Interview with Jornal de Negocios
articles.extracted_text: The European Parliament gave the digital euro the green light last week. Do any major hurdles still remain or is the way clear for the project to be implemented? There are currently three versions of the draft legislation: the European Commission’s, the Council’s – representing the joint position of Member States – and the Parliament’s. The three institutions will already start negotiating this month to reach an agreement on the final text. We hope the text will be finalised by the end of the year, at which point we’ll be in a position to take a decision on the future issuance of the digital euro. The vote in the European Parliament last week was a very important milestone, particularly because some lawmakers were initially more critical of the Commission’s proposal. However, the text that was ultimately approved aligns closely with the approach taken by the Commission and the Council. We hope the three-way negotiations will proceed without any major difficulties. Is the ECB satisfied with the stance adopted by the European Parliament? Generally speaking, yes. The text largely aligns with the approach outlined in the Commission’s initial proposal. There are some aspects we would like to see reinforced, such as the requirement for payment service providers to support the digital euro app that the ECB is developing. This app will ensure universal access, resilience in exceptional circumstances and a level of accessibility that goes above and beyond European accessibility standards. In keeping with the Commission’s proposal, we would like the final text to ensure that all Europeans will always have the option of using this app. The digital euro has been viewed by some political groups in the European Parliament as a divisive project. Do you think these differences of opinion will be overcome during the negotiations? I don’t agree with the claim that it’s a divisive project. The digital euro was approved by almost 70% of European Parliament members. That’s anything but divisive. And in the EU Council, all the Member States agreed on a common position. We are dealing with a highly transformative project and that’s why it’s important that it has strong democratic support. In designing the digital euro, we have aimed to address all the concerns that have been expressed in the legislative process. We are working to make sure the digital euro works for everyone, with the highest standards of privacy and accessibility. The digital euro will be a digital form of cash, complementing the euro banknotes and coins that bring Europeans together. If the text is not approved by the end of the year, does the ECB have room to accommodate that delay without having to redesign the project? We aim to be ready for first issuance in 2029, assuming the legislative process is completed by the end of 2026. We have always said that if this process is delayed, we will have to take another look at the timetable, not the project. In any case, we will only take a decision on issuance of the digital euro once the legislation has been adopted. The technical side of the project is advancing fast and under our current timeline we will launch the pilot phase in 2027. The pilot will be a key chance to test the digital euro design and gather feedback. It will be the first step, followed by additional steps focused on scaling up and developing functionalities in preparation for the issuance. 1.Bulletin on the National Summer Grain Output in 2026
articles.extracted_text: According to the survey conducted across 25 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities) engaged in summer grain production nationwide, the sown area, yield per unit area and total output of summer grain in China for 2026 are as follows: I. The total sown area of summer grain nationwide recorded 26,532.4 thousand hectares (397.986 million mu), a decrease of 46.0 thousand hectares (690 thousand mu) or 0.2% compared to 2025. Among them, the sown area of wheat recorded 23,006.8 thousand hectares (345.102 million mu), a decrease of 66.6 thousand hectares (998 thousand mu) or 0.3% compared to 2025. II. The yield per unit area of summer grain nationwide recorded 5,681.6 kilograms per hectare (378.8 kilograms per mu), an increase of 47.5 kilograms per hectare (3.2 kilograms per mu) or 0.8% compared to 2025. Among them, the yield per unit area of wheat recorded 6,039.6 kilograms per hectare (402.6 kilograms per mu), an increase of 51.4 kilograms per hectare (3.4 kilograms per mu) or 0.9% compared to 2025. III. The total output of summer grain nationwide recorded 150.746 million tons (301.49 billion jin), an increase of 1,000 thousand tons (2.00 billion jin) or 0.7% compared to 2025. Among them, the total output of wheat recorded 138.952 million tons (277.90 billion jin), an increase of 783 thousand tons (1.57 billion jin) or 0.6% compared to 2025. 1. The wheat harvest in some regions of Gansu, Ningxia and Xinjiang has not yet been completed, so the data provided for these three provinces and regions are yield estimates and the actual yields shall be subject to the forthcomingâ China Rural Statistical Yearbook 2026 . 2. Since some data in this table are rounded off, discrepancies may exist between the total and the sum of data of all provinces, regions and municipalities. Explanation of the Yield Survey of Summer Grain The yield data of summer grain released by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) of China were collected through two survey methods: sampling surveys conducted in major summer grain-producing areas, with each province treated as a separate survey population, and key-point surveys conducted in non-major producing areas. Summer grain includes grains, beans and tubers harvested in summer. Among them, the grains harvested in summer mainly include wheat, barley, oat, buckwheat, etc.; beans harvested in summer mainly include broad beans, peas, etc.; and tubers harvested in summer mainly include potatoes and sweet potatoes. The summer grain output data of 16 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities), including Beijing, Tianjin, Hebei, Shanxi, Jiangsu, Anhui, Shandong, Henan, Hubei, Sichuan, Guizhou, Yunnan, Shaanxi, Gansu, Ningxia and Xinjiang, were obtained through sampling surveys and actual harvesting measurements organized by the respective survey teams of the NBS in over 400 selected national survey counties (cities). The output data of summer grain from nine provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities), including Shanghai, Zhejiang, Fujian, Jiangxi, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan and Chongqing, were estimated according to key-point surveys conducted by relevant survey teams. The sampling survey for the yield of summer grain is composed of a sampling survey for the sown area and a sampling survey for the yield per unit area. articles.title: 2.Consumer Price Index in June 2026
articles.extracted_text: In June 2026, Chinaâs Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by 1.0% year on year. Specifically, the price index in urban areas increased by 1.0% while that in rural areas increased by 0.8%; the price index for food decreased by 1.6% while that for non-food increased by 1.5%; the price index for consumer goods increased by 1.1% while that for services increased by 0.8%. From January to June, on average, Chinaâs CPI increased by 1.0% year on year. In June, Chinaâs CPI decreased by 0.3% month on month. Specifically, the price index in urban areas decreased by 0.4% while that in rural areas decreased by 0.3%; the price index for food decreased by 0.4% while that for non-food decreased by 0.3%; the price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.6% while that for services remained flat. I. Year-on-Year Changes in Price Indexes for Various Goods and Services In June, the price index for food, tobacco, liquor and dining out decreased by 0.8% year on year, bringing the CPI down by about 0.24 percentage points. Among food, the price index for meat of livestock decreased by 7.3%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.30 percentage points, of which the price index for pork decreased by 15.9%; that for milk and other dairy products decreased by 1.7%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.02 percentage points; that for fresh fruits decreased by 0.7%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.01 percentage points; that for eggs increased by 16.0%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.08 percentage points. Among the price indexes for the other seven major categories, six increased, and one decreased year on year. Specifically, the price indexes for miscellaneous goods and services, transportation and telecommunication, and health care and medical services increased by 6.6%, 4.1% and 2.3%, respectively; the price indexes for clothing and education, culture and recreation both increased by 1.4%; the price index for household facilities, articles and services increased by 1.3%; and the price index for residence decreased by 0.3%. II. Month-on-Month Changes in Price Indexes for Various Goods and Services In June, the price index for food, tobacco, liquor and dining out decreased by 0.3% month on month, bringing the CPI down by about 0.08 percentage points. Among food, the price index for fresh fruits decreased by 2.0%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.04 percentage points; that for fresh vegetables decreased by 1.0%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.02 percentage points; that for meat of livestock decreased by 0.5%, bringing the CPI down by about 0.02 percentage points, of which the price index for pork decreased by 0.8%; and that for eggs increased by 5.8%, pushing the CPI up by about 0.03 percentage points. Among the price indexes for the other seven major categories, one increased, two remained flatâ and four decreasedâ month on month. Specifically, the price index for health care and medical services increased by 0.2%; the price indexes for residence and education, culture and recreation remained flat; the price indexes for miscellaneous goods and services and transportation and telecommunication decreased by 2.7% and 1.3%, respectively; the price indexes for household facilities, articles and services and clothing decreased by 0.2% and 0.1%, respectively. articles.title: 3.Industrial Producer Price Indexes in June 2026
articles.extracted_text: In June 2026, Chinaâs producer price index for industrial products (PPI) increased by 4.1% year on year and decreased by 0.3% month on month. The purchasing price index for industrial producers increased by 6.4% year on year and decreased by 0.2% month on month.â In the first half of the year, the producer price index for industrial products increased by 1.5% year on year, and the purchasing price index for industrial producers increased by 2.4%. I. Year-on-Year Changes in Industrial Producer Price Indexes In June, among producer price indexes for industrial products, the price index for means of production increased by 5.5% year on year, pushing the overall producer price index for industrial products up by about 4.28 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for mining and quarrying increased by 16.5%, that for raw materials increased by 8.6%, and that for the processing industry increased by 3.0%. The price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.9%, bringing the overall producer price index for industrial products down by about 0.20 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for food decreased by 2.1%, that for clothing and articles for daily use both decreased by 1.0%, and that for durable consumer goods increased by 0.1%. Among purchasing price indexes for industrial producers, the price index for nonferrous metals and cables increased by 21.6%, that for fuel and power increased by 11.8%, that for raw chemical materials increased by 11.5%, that for textile materials increased by 3.3%, that for ferrous metals increased by 1.3%, that for building materials and non-metals decreased by 4.8%, and that for agricultural and sideline products decreased by 1.3%. II. Month-on-Month Changes in Industrial Producer Price Indexes In June, among producer price indexes for industrial products, the price index for means of production decreased by 0.3% month on month, bringing the overall producer price index for industrial products down by about 0.25 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for mining and quarrying decreased by 0.9%, that for raw materials decreased by 1.2%, and that for the processing industry increased by 0.2%. The price index for consumer goods decreased by 0.3%, bringing the overall producer price index for industrial products down by about 0.06 percentage points. Specifically, the price index for food decreased by 0.8%, that for clothing and durable consumer goods both remained flat, and that for articles for daily use decreased by 0.1%. Among purchasing price indexes for industrial producers, the price index for raw chemical materials decreased by 1.3%, that for fuel and power decreased by 0.5%, that for building materials and non-metals decreased by 0.4%, that for agricultural and sideline products decreased by 0.3%, that for textile materials increased by 0.7%, that for nonferrous metals and cables increased by 0.1%, and that for ferrous metals remained flat. articles.extracted_text: China Economic Monitoring and Analysis Center In late June 2026, according to the monitoring of market prices of 50 kinds of important means of production under 9 categories in the national circulation field, the prices of 13 products increased, 34 kinds decreased, and 3 kinds remained flat compared with those in mid-June 2026. Market prices of important means of production in circulation refer to the wholesale prices and selling prices of enterprises trading important means of production. Different from producer prices, market prices of means of production include producer prices, as well as circulation expenses, profits, and taxes of the enterprises. Producer prices and market prices influence each other. Due to a time lag, their movements may show divergence in a certain period of time. The monitoring of market prices of important means of production in circulation includes the prices of 50 kinds of products under 9 categories. For details of product categories and specifications, please see the attached table. The monitoring covers nearly 2,000 wholesalers, agents, and dealers of more than 300 trading markets in 31 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities). Price monitoring methods include information officers gathering prices on-site or requesting prices by telephone, instant messaging tools, e-mail, and so on. The number of products with price increases, decreases, and no change is counted based on the percentage change (%). articles.title: 5.Purchasing Managersâ Index for June 2026
articles.extracted_text: Department of Service Statistics of NBS of China China Federation of Logistics & Purchasing (CFLP) I. Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index In June, the purchasing managersâ index (PMI) of Chinaâs manufacturing industry was 50.3%, an increase of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month and back to the expansionary range. By enterprise size, the PMI for large-sized enterprises was 50.7%, a decrease of 0.4 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for medium sized enterprises was 50.5%, an increase of 1.9 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for small-sized enterprises was 48.2%, a decrease of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month, which was below the threshold. By sub-index, among the five sub-indexes that constitute the manufacturing PMI, the production index and the new order index were both above the threshold, while the raw materials inventory index, the employment index and the supplier delivery time index were all below the threshold. The production index was 51.4%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating an accelerated expansion in production activities in the manufacturing industry. The new order index was 51.2%, an increase of 1.3 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a recovery in the market demand of the manufacturing industry. The raw materials inventory index was 48.4%, a decrease of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a continued decline in the inventories of main raw materials in the manufacturing industry. The employment index was 48.5%, a decrease of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a slight decline in the employment climate of the manufacturing industry. The supplier delivery time index was 49.9%, an increase of 0.7 percentage points from the previous month, and still below the threshold, indicating a slight slowdown in raw material delivery times among manufacturing suppliers. Table 1 Chinaâs Manufacturing PMI and Sub-indexes (Seasonally Adjusted) Table 2 Related Indexes of Chinaâs Manufacturing PMI (Seasonally Adjusted) Production and Operation Expectation Index II. Non-manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index In June, the non-manufacturing business activity index was 50.2%, an increase of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a recovery in the climate of non-manufacturing industries. By industry, the business activity index for the construction industry was 49.0%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month; that for the service industry was 50.4%, an increase of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month. In terms of the service industry, the business activity indexes for telecommunication, radio, television and satellite transmission services, Internet software and information technology services, monetary and financial services and insurance were all in the relatively high climate range of above 55.0%; those for civil aviation transportation and real estate were all below the threshold. The new order index was 48.0%, an increase of 3.0 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a significant improvement in the market demand climate of the non-manufacturing industry. articles.title: 6.Report on the Development of Culture and Related Industries in 2025
articles.extracted_text: In 2025, under the strong leadership of the Party Central Committee with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and plans of the Party Central Committee and the State Council. Centered on the goal of building China into a country with a strong culture, the country moved faster to nurture new-type cultural enterprises and new cultural forms, optimized the business environment, and improved the quality of development. Cultural and related industries achieved vigorous growth with steadily rising profitability. Industries with new cultural forms took the lead in driving development, and scientific and technological innovation in cultural industries picked up speed. i. Business revenue of culture and related industries exceeded 20 trillion yuan With the continuous improvement of Chinaâs cultural industrial system and market system, Chinaâs culture and related industries (hereinafter referred to as âcultural industriesâ) have expanded steadily. In 2025, cultural industries nationwide achieved a business revenue of 20,825.4 billion yuan [1] , surpassing the 20 trillion yuan threshold and hitting a record high, up by 8.8% year on year [2] . Underpinned by culture-related services, the structure of cultural industries kept improving. In 2025, culture-related services achieved a business revenue of 12,305.2 billion yuan, up by 12.8% year on year, accounting for 59.1% of the total business revenue of cultural industries, up by 2.1 percentage points year on year. Culture-related services contributed 82.7% to the business revenue growth of cultural industries. Culture-related manufacturing and culture-related wholesale and retail achieved business revenue of 4,991.9 billion yuan and 3,528.3 billion yuan, respectively, up by 0.6% and 8.0% year on year. ii. Business revenue of nine major sectors of cultural industries achieved growth across the board In 2025, business revenue saw growth across nine major sectors of cultural industries. Specifically, four sectors posted double-digit business revenue growth year on year: creative design services (13.2%), content creation and production (12.4%), news and information services (12.3%), and cultural, entertainment and leisure services (10.7%). The other five sectors recorded year-on-year growth rates as follows: 8.0% in cultural equipment production, 7.7% in cultural communication channels, 3.1% in cultural investment and operation, 2.7% in cultural auxiliary production and intermediary services, and 2.5% in cultural consumption terminal production. By region, cultural industries in eastern and western regions achieved business revenue of 15,528.0 billion yuan and 2,029.8 billion yuan, up by 9.5% and 9.9% year on year, respectively, both higher than the national average growth rate. The central and northeastern regions achieved business revenue of 3,007.1 billion yuan and 260.5 billion yuan, up by 4.7% and 4.8% year on year. i. Total assets of cultural industries kept rising At the end of 2025, the total assets of Chinaâs cultural industries reached 37,447.4 billion yuan, an increase of 8.9% over the end of the previous year. By industry type, total assets of culture-related manufacturing, culture-related wholesale and retail, and culture-related services were 5,060.2 billion yuan, 2,744.3 billion yuan and 29,642.8 billion yuan, respectively, up by 5.7%, 7.2% and 9.6% over the end of the previous year. articles.title: 7.Profits of Industrial Enterprises above the Designated Size from January to May in 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total profits of industrial enterprises above the designated size nationwide reached 3,143.96 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 18.8% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 2 for details). From January to May, among industrial enterprises above the designated size, state-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 1,048.66 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 19.6%; share-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 2,434.81 billion yuan, an increase of 24.1%; enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan realized a total profit of 695.72 billion yuan, an increase of 4.2%; private enterprises realized a total profit of 772.65 billion yuan, an increase of 10.7%. From January to May, the mining industry realized a total profit of 479.52 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 33.5%; the manufacturing industry realized a total profit of 2,328.52 billion yuan, an increase of 20.0%; the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water realized a total profit of 335.92 billion yuan, a decrease of 2.7%. From January to May, the profits of main industries were as follows: the profit of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals increased by 117.1% year on year, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment increased by 103.9%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products increased by 71.6%, that of the mining and washing of coal increased by 33.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas increased by 17.2%, that of the textile industry increased by 11.7%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery decreased by 0.2%, that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power decreased by 4.1%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery decreased by 5.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products decreased by 13.3%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus decreased by 13.7%, that of the manufacture of automobiles decreased by 19.8%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals decreased by 37.4%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products decreased by 48.9%, and the processing of petroleum, coal and other fuels turned from loss to profit. From January to May, industrial enterprises above the designated size achieved a business revenue of 56.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.5%; the operating costs incurred were 48.04 trillion yuan, an increase of 4.7%; the profit rate of business revenue was 5.56%, a year-on-year increase of 0.63 percentage points. At the end of May, the assets of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 193.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.8%; the liabilities totaled 112.69 trillion yuan, an increase of 6.5%; the ownersâ equity totaled 80.87 trillion yuan, an increase of 5.0%; the asset-liability ratio was 58.2%, a year-on-year increase of 0.3 percentage points. At the end of May, the accounts receivable of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 28.17 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 7.7%; the inventory of finished goods was 7.14 trillion yuan, an increase of 8.8%. From January to May, the costs for per-hundred-yuan business revenue of industrial enterprises above the designated size were 84.95 yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 0.59 yuan; the expenses for per-hundred-

articles.title: 8.Market Prices of Important Means of Production in Circulation, June 11-20, 2026
articles.extracted_text: China Economic Monitoring and Analysis Center In mid-June 2026, according to the monitoring of market prices of 50 kinds of important means of production under 9 categories in the national circulation field, the prices of 15 products increased, 32 kinds decreased, and 3 kinds remained flat compared with those in early June 2026. Market prices of important means of production in circulation refer to the wholesale prices and selling prices of enterprises trading important means of production. Different from producer prices, market prices of means of production include producer prices, as well as circulation expenses, profits, and taxes of the enterprises. Producer prices and market prices influence each other. Due to a time lag, their movements may show divergence in a certain period of time. The monitoring of market prices of important means of production in circulation includes the prices of 50 kinds of products under 9 categories. For details of product categories and specifications, please see the attached table. The monitoring covers nearly 2,000 wholesalers, agents, and dealers of more than 300 trading markets in 31 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities). Price monitoring methods include information officers gathering prices on-site or requesting prices by telephone, instant messaging tools, e-mail, and so on. The number of products with price increases, decreases, and no change is counted based on the percentage change (%). articles.title: 9.Industrial Production Operation in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 4.5% year on year in real terms (the growth rates of value added are the real growth rates after deducting price factors), 0.4 percentage points higher than the previous month. On a month-on-month basis, in May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 0.40% over the previous month. From January to May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 5.4% year on year. In terms of three sectors, in May, the value added of the mining industry increased by 2.3% year on year, that of the manufacturing industry increased by 4.4%, and that of the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water increased by 7.6%. In terms of economic types, in May, the value added of state-holding enterprises increased by 3.7% year on year; that of share-holding enterprises increased by 5.2%; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan increased by 1.9%; and that of private enterprises increased by 2.7%. In terms of industries, in May, the value added of 28 out of 41 major industries continued to show year-on-year growth. Among them, the value added of the mining and washing of coal went up by 3.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas up by 1.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products up by 1.5%, that of the manufacture of liquor, beverages and refined tea down by 2.7%, that of the textile industry up by 2.6%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products up by 0.3%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products down by 5.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals up by 1.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals down by 4.5%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery up by 6.7%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery up by 9.1%, that of the manufacture of automobiles up by 8.3%, that of the manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment up by 7.4%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus up by 4.7%, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment up by 17.0%, and that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power up by 8.7%. In terms of products, in May, the output of 300 out of 626 products produced by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased year on year. Among them, the output of rolled steel was 123.03 million tons, down by 2.8% year on year; that of cement was 149.91 million tons, down by 8.1%; that of ten kinds of non-ferrous metals was 6.98 million tons, up by 2.2%; that of ethylene was 3.38 million tons, up by 2.1%; that of motor vehicles was 2.582 million sets, down by 3.2%, including 1.489 million new energy vehicles, up by 17.8%; the electricity generation was 784.3 billion kWh, up by 4.2%; and the crude oil processing volume was 53.72 million tons, down by 9.1%. In May, the product sales ratio of industrial enterprises above the designated size was 96.0%, a year-on-year decrease of 0.1 percentage points; and the export delivery value realized by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1,388.4 billion yuan, a nominal increase of 10.1% year on year. articles.title: 10.Investment in Fixed Assets from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May 2026, the national investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) was 17,851.2 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 4.1% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 7 for details). Specifically, the investment in intellectual property products increased by 9.3% year on year. By industry, the investment in the primary industry was 388.7 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.9%; the investment in the secondary industry was 6,498.9 billion yuan, up 0.1%; and the investment in the tertiary industry was 10,963.6 billion yuan, down 6.8%. The industrial investment increased by 0.1% year on year. Specifically, the investment in mining increased by 6.2%, the investment in manufacturing decreased by 0.4%, and the investment in production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water decreased by 0.1%. The investment in infrastructure (see Annotation 1 for details of statistical scope) increased by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the investment in waterway transportation increased by 23.3%, the investment in civil aviation transportation increased by 21.7%, the investment in ecological conservation and environmental governance increased by 3.5%, and the investment in the production and supply of gas increased by 2.8%. By region, the investment in the eastern region decreased by 6.4% year on year, the investment in the central region decreased by 2.9%, the investment in the western region decreased by 6.6%, and the investment in the northeastern region decreased by 17.5%. By statistical category of registration, the investment in fixed assets of domestic-invested enterprises decreased by 3.9% year on year, the investment in fixed assets of enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan decreased by 8.7%, and the investment in fixed assets of foreign-invested enterprises decreased by 4.3%. From January to May 2026, non-governmental investment in fixed assets decreased by 7.1% year on year. On a month-on-month basis, the investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) decreased by 1.91% in May. Investment in Fixed Assets (Excluding Rural Households) from January to May 2026 Investment in Fixed Assets (Excluding Rural Households) Agriculture, forestry, animal husbandry and fishery Of which: Processing of food from agricultural and sideline products Manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products Smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals Manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment Manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus Manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment Production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water Water conservancy, environment and public facilities management Of which: Management of water conservancy Enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan Note: The growth rates in this table are not adjusted for price factors. articles.title: 11.Investment in Real Estate Development from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: I. The Completion of Investment in Real Estate Development From January to May, the investment in real estate development was 3,035.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 16.2% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 5 for details), of which the investment in residential buildings was 2,342.6 billion yuan, down 15.6%. From January to May, the floor space of buildings under construction by real estate development enterprises was 5,487.75 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 12.3%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings under construction was 3,808.30 million square meters, down 12.6%. The floor space of buildings newly started was 179.29 million square meters, down 22.6%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings newly started was 130.84 million square meters, down 23.4%. The floor space of buildings completed was 140.87 million square meters, down 23.4%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings completed was 99.99 million square meters, down 25.0%. II. Sales of Newly Built Commercial Buildings From January to May, the floor space of newly built commercial buildings sold was 313.20 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 10.8%, of which the floor space of residential buildings sold decreased by 12.1%. The sales of newly built commercial buildings were 2,936.6 billion yuan, down 13.5%, of which the sales of residential buildings decreased by 14.1%. At the end of May, the floor space of commercial buildings for sale was 771.82 million square meters, down 0.4% year on year. Specifically, the floor space for sale held for less than three years was 571.52 million square meters, down 2.8%. III. Funds for Investment This Year for Real Estate Development Enterprises From January to May, the funds for investment this year for real estate development enterprises were 3,275.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 19.0%. Among them, domestic loans were 487.5 billion yuan, down 28.7%; self-raised funds were 1,198.5 billion yuan, down 13.0%; deposits and advance receipts were 1,001.2 billion yuan, down 16.1%; individual mortgage loans were 406.6 billion yuan, down 28.0%. articles.title: Meeting with Acting President of South Ossetia Marat Kambolov
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin received acting President of the Republic of South Ossetia Marat Kambolov at the Kremlin.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin received acting President of the Republic of South Ossetia Marat Kambolov at the Kremlin. President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Mr Kambolov, first and foremost, allow me to congratulate you. To begin with, on your initial step, namely your premiership: the republic’s parliament has expressed its support for your appointment. Your predecessor, in fact, acted in accordance with his intentions – which we had discussed – and put forward your nomination prior to accepting my proposal to assume the post of Adviser in the Presidential Executive Office. Alan Gagloyev recommended you. I sincerely trust that you, too, will build upon his achievements, draw upon his experience, and rely on his authority within the republic in the course of your own work. As regards future prospects, I am confident that your own considerable – indeed, I would say – experience within the federal authorities of the Russian Federation, in ministries and agencies, as well as in academia, will enable you to set the correct priorities for the republic’s development and to implement them effectively. I have no doubt in this regard. I would, of course, wish you every success in the forthcoming electoral events in South Ossetia. I hope that the residents of the republic will lend you their support, and that you, in turn, will prove worthy of their trust. For our part, we will take every step to support you. Naturally, we understand that a number of issues and challenges remain. Yet I must stress that both you and your family consider the republic your home land, and I am certain that you will devote your utmost energies to its advancement. The republic and the people of South Ossetia – they are, indeed, a special people: warriors, scholars, and workers. As I have already stated, we will provide every possible support. Today, we have an opportunity to discuss the priorities for South Ossetia’s development that are, one might say, self-evident, and perhaps to jointly seek solutions to the tasks confronting the republic. Acting President of South Ossetia Marat Kambolov: Thank you. First of all, I wish to thank you for your particular regard for the people of South Ossetia. I must say that the events of August 2008, when the Russian Federation stood in defence of South Ossetia, are something we will never forget. As for our people, their affiliate themselves with the Russian Federation. In every event and in every issue we discuss, the role and significance of the Russian Federation for South Ossetia are fairly important. I will inform you in detail about the issues I would like to raise. Vladimir Putin: Let us address the priorities now.

articles.title: Popular Front’s Everything for Victory! forum
articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin attended the Everything for Victory! forum organised by the Russian Popular Front public movement, and presented awards to the winners of the Popular Front’s prize.
articles.extracted_text: All content on this site is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International Vladimir Putin attended the Everything for Victory! forum organised by the Russian Popular Front public movement, and presented awards to the winners of the Popular Front’s prize. The President presented awards to security guards Anatoly Vasilchenko and Viktor Taraskin for assisting students in Starobelsk following a terrorist attack by the Ukrainian Armed Forces. He also awarded Shamil Ustarbekov for saving people during a flash flood in Daghestan and the Pojiarskas family from the Amur Region for establishing a group of volunteers to assist service personnel taking part in the special military operation. Ahead of the awards ceremony, held on the territory of the Rossiya National Centre, Vladimir Putin viewed an exhibition of projects of the Russian Popular Front. Head of the Front’s Executive Committee Mikhail Kuznetsov Kuznetsov Mikhail Head of the Russian Popular Front Executive Committee acted as guide. The President was shown inventions of the Kulibin Club, an association of enterprises of the people’s defence industry, briefed on the Front’s assistance to people in Russian border regions and liberated territories. The President also inspected stands showcasing the rehabilitation of veterans of the special military operation, as well as other exhibits highlighting mobile medicine and programmes for training drone operators. First Deputy Chief of Staff of the Presidential Executive Office Sergei Kiriyenko Kiriyenko Sergei First Deputy Chief of Staff of the Presidential Executive Office , Minister of Industry and Trade Anton Alikhanov Alikhanov Anton Minister of Industry and Trade of the Russian Federation , and Deputy Defence Minister Alexei Krivoruchko attended the forum along with the President. Address at the Everything for Victory! awards ceremony President of Russia Vladimir Putin: Thank you very much for such a warm welcome. For my part, I would like to say that when the Russian Popular Front was established 15 years ago, it was by no means a spontaneous decision. Today, as we mark its 15 th anniversary, I can say with confidence that it has fully justified its creation. First and foremost, it has brought together people like you. I want to express my deepest gratitude for everything you do. Over the past 15 years – not just during the years of the special military operation, which is highly important, and I will certainly mention it as well – you have always been at the forefront of addressing the country’s challenges. You have always been able to identify where attention should be focused in order to help specific people. Ultimately, that means helping the entire country and the Russian state as a whole. This direct feedback from people is invaluable for every level of government and for the country. As I understand it, you are currently monitoring the implementation of 552 presidential instructions. These are not abstract discussions. Where do presidential instructions come from? They emerge from the Direct Line with citizens. Of course, these direct lines are carefully prepared and supported by large teams, but they nevertheless represent a live dialogue with the people. These instructions do not appear out of nowhere. They arise directly from people’s concerns – from the problems they face in their daily lives and the challenges they are striving to overcome. That is where they come from. And what are you doing? You are working to h

articles.raw_summary: Vladimir Putin wished a happy birthday to ballet dancer, People’s Artist of Russia Diana Vishneva
